titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lee Anderson Papers and Yale Series of Recorded Poets Sound Recordings
Anderson (Lee) Papers and Yale Series of Recorded Poets Sound Recordings
titlestmt.author: by Molly Wheeler

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 6, 2019 at 04:09

did.unittitle: Lee Anderson papers and Yale Series of Recorded Poets sound recordings

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Lee Anderson Papers and Yale Series of Recorded Poets Sound Recordings document the work of poet and editor Lee Anderson with the Yale Series of Recorded Poets. The bulk of the sound recordings consist of poetry readings by various poets and a small number contain poets' lectures on poetry. The papers consist of writings, correspondence, contracts, lists, procedural documentation, and printed material relating to the series production and publication.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Lee Anderson, September 1974 and Washington University, 1980.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: All sound recordings in this collection are duplicates of sound recordings held by the Yale Collection of Historical Sound Recordings (HSR). As Lee Anderson made sound recordings for the Yale Series of Recorded Poets in the 1950s and 1960s, he sent the original tapes to HSR for their collection. Anderson's 1974 gift to the Beinecke Library originally included 696 sound recordings. These recordings were sent to HSR for comparison of the material held by HSR. HSR retained the originals, to complete their collection of original recordings, and duplicates were returned to the Beinecke Library. Those returned duplicates comprise this collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 1-17: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Lee Anderson Papers and Yale Series of Recorded Poets Sound Recordings is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lee Anderson Papers and Yale Series of Recorded Poets Sound Recordings. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call numbers: Uncat MSS 380 and Za Uncat MSS Anderson.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Original sound recordings are held at Yale Collection of Historical Sound Recordings. Please see Provenance note for more information.
Selections of sound recordings have been digitized and are available through PennSound at http://writing.upenn.edu/pennsound.

bioghist.head: Lee Anderson
bioghist.p: Lee Anderson was born in Saxton, Pennsylvania and attended the University of Pittsburgh in 1896. He served in the U.S. Navy during World War I and spent several years as a self-employed printer and farmer in western Pennsylvania. In 1942, he published his collection of poetry, Prevailing Winds . Anderson became interested in poetry readings and the oral aspects of poetry and began to record contemporary poets reading their work onto audiotape. At first, Anderson kept the recordings for his personal collection but soon he was asked by the Library of Congress to record and submit recorded poetry readings to the library. He submitted approximately 100 tapes to the library.

In 1955, Yale University acquired Anderson's complete collection of more than 500 tapes of contemporary British and American poets reading from their work, including duplicates of recordings donated to the Library of Congress. In addition, Yale hired Anderson as a Research Associate. While at Yale, he helped produce the Yale Series of Recorded Poets, a collection of LP records of poets reading their work which was derived largely from Anderson's tape collection. Anderson made additional recordings of poets reading while he was at Yale. In addition to producing recordings, Anderson edited several books in his lifetime.

After leaving Yale, Anderson taught at several institutions and continued to write and lecture. He continued recording but primarily recorded his work. In the late 1960's Anderson became ill. He died in Florida in 1972.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Lee Anderson Papers and Yale Series of Recorded Poets Sound Recordings document the work of poet and editor Lee Anderson in recording contemporary poets reading from their work and later producing the Yale Series of Recorded Poets. The bulk of the sound recordings consist of poetry readings by various poets and a small number of poets' lectures on poetry. The papers consist of writings, correspondence, contracts, lists, procedural documentation, and printed material relating to the series production and publication. Included among the papers is Lee Anderson's notebook draft of "Memoirs," containing observations of poets reading for the Yale Series of Record Poets. The sound recordings and papers span the years 1951 to 1968.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Sound Recordings, 1951-1968. II. Papers, 1951-1968.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All sound recordings are 7 inch 1/4" reel-to-reel tape formats unless otherwise noted. Numbers in brackets represent number of reels.

did.unittitle: The Mike Wallace Interview, Guest: Sessue Hayakawa

did.unittitle: Typescript and handwritten drafts of poems from various poets
Typescript and handwritten drafts of poems from various poets
"Memoirs," three manuscript notebooks containing Anderson's observations of poets reading for the Yale Series of Recorded Poets
"Memoirs," one manuscript notebook containing Anderson's observations of poets reading for the Yale Series of Recorded Poets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Between Yale University and Lee Anderson, between Yale University and Poetry London-New York

did.unittitle: Outline of procedures for editors of the Yale Poetry series
Budget for broadcast of Yale Series of Recorded Poets over NAEB and other outlets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes lists for the Lee Anderson Collection of Recorded Poets and a list of poems selected for recording by Richard Eberhart, Robert Frost, and Edwin Honig

did.unittitle: Proposal for the production of literature phonograph records by Yale University
Editorial material from the Yale Series of Recorded Poets
Eberhert, R. Correspondence concerning his recorded poetry
Hollander, John. Correspondence, poetry drafts and printed material regarding recorded poetry
Scott, Winfield T. Correspondence concerning his recorded poetry; handwritten and typed drafts and page proofs of poetry, corrected
Tate, Allan. Correspondence regarding his recorded poetry
Waith, Eugene M. Yale Series of Recorded Poets page proofs, corrected
Warnke, Frank J. Yale Series of Recorded Poets, Louis MacNeice typescript draft, corrected
Warren, R.P. Correspondence concerning his recorded poetry; handwritten and typescript drafts; printed material; and page proof, corrected
Memos, notes and clippings regarding the Yale Series of Recorded Poets Sound Recordings
Printed material and pamphlets to accompany recordings
List of Lee Anderson correspondents, photocopies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Available reference copies are noted in the listing above.
